other_observers: Andrew Spencer, Nathan Pieplow, and Earl (Gene) Wild (my grandpa).; Sandy Fliniau found the bird before we showed up to the house and then she showed it to us.
specific_location: Sandy Fliniau's House in the east side of Walden. I don't know the address of her house, but it is close to the school. It was just sitting in the tree. She has a lot of feeders and a fish pond with running water in her back yard.
description: A light colored Dove, not as light as Ringed Turtle-Dove. It had a black band on the back of the neck. It had dark primaries and a light brownish wash on the back. The undertail coverts were gray and it had dark feathers going down the sides of the undersides of the tail for about an inch or two in length. It had a square shaped tail. It was a heavy looking dove. I never saw it fly.Behaviors: It was sitting in a tree next to Sandy Fliniau's House in Walden. It looked cold (feathers fluffed) since it was a cold December day.Call: Didn't hear itPlumage: Adult
